hypothesis: If continual pre-training of a large language model on MOF-related articles and PubChem data followed by supervised fine-tuning on MOF property datasets using MOFid textual representations leads to lower mean absolute error in MOF property predictions than baselines (CGCNN, SOAP, MOFormer), especially at low-data regimes (1K–10K samples), because the pre-training imbues domain-specific knowledge that enables effective text-based MOF property prediction.
